incident_reconstruction.impact_direction: Frontal impact, likely at an angle given the damage to one side of the front bumper and headlight
incident_reconstruction.reconstruction: The vehicle likely swerved to avoid another vehicle or object and collided with a tree, causing a frontal impact that resulted in severe damage to the front bumper, moderate damage to the bonnet, and moderate damage to the left headlight.
reconstruction_bullets: The damage to the front bumper, bonnet, and left headlight is consistent with a frontal collision.
The severity of the damage suggests a significant impact, possibly at a speed that caused considerable force to be applied to the front of the vehicle.
The absence of damage to other parts of the vehicle suggests that the impact was primarily frontal and not a sideswipe or rollover.

storyboard_panels.title: Pre-Incident
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle was traveling on a road when the vehicle ahead stopped suddenly.

storyboard_panels.title: Point of Impact
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle swerved to avoid the stopped vehicle and collided with a tree.

storyboard_panels.title: Immediate Aftermath
storyboard_panels.description: The driver assessed the damage and stopped to inspect the vehicle.

storyboard_panels.title: Evidence Summary
storyboard_panels.description: The physical evidence shows a frontal impact consistent with the described incident.

incident_reconstruction.summary: Most Probable: Front impact | Confidence: 80% | Story Match: Yes

decision_reasons: Fraud score of 66 is high and indicates potential fraud, although it does not exceed the critical threshold of 70
Policy expiry date (2026-04-01) is 68 days before the incident date (2026-06-08), making the claim ineligible for coverage as per policy terms
No FIR uploaded for the 'Motor - Third Party' claim, which is mandatory for such incidents (FI-CLM-003)
Plate visible in the image (WB14L0428) may not match the policy vehicle (Toyota Innova Crysta GX (TN07CD5678)), indicating a potential vehicle substitution
reasoning_trail: Step 1: Damage Assessment indicates significant front-end damage consistent with a collision, but this is not sufficient to approve the claim due to other red flags
Step 2: Fraud Intelligence reveals a high fraud score and several indicators of potential fraud, including the policy expiry date and lack of FIR
Step 3: Incident Reconstruction confirms that the damage matches the story, but this is not enough to outweigh the other concerns
Step 4: Context & Policy verification shows that the policy had expired, making the claim ineligible for coverage
Step 5: Historical Precedents do not provide a direct match, but the high fraud score and policy expiry date are significant concerns
Step 6: Settlement Calculation is not applicable due to the rejection of the claim
Step 7: Final Decision is to reject the claim based on the high fraud score, policy expiry date, and lack of FIR
